rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total>=1 -> FAIL
candidate_llm_application: BAHAL-PROCESS-LAYER: shared append-only process-context log for multi-agent LLMs; all agents read full process telemetry before any consensus decision; continuous process-level transparency (not opinion-level).

results.title: LLM Multi-Agent Blackboard System Info Discovery Data Science (2025/10)
results.justification: DIRECT TWIN — globally accessible blackboard as sole memory + message hub; every agent reads entire blackboard before acting and posts entries. This is the exact artefact of BAHAL-PROCESS-LAYER (shared append-only log + all-agents-read-before-decision).

results.title: Advanced LLM Multi-Agent Blackboard Architecture (2025/07)
results.justification: bMAS framework with persistent blackboard communication; functionally identical to the proposed shared process-context log.

results.title: AgentTrace Structured Logging Framework Observability (2026/02)
results.justification: AgentTrace's cognitive/operational/contextual 3-surface taxonomy directly subsumes BAHAL's process-level telemetry (tool-calls, beliefs, failures, hypothesis) as shared context.

results.title: Semantic Consensus Framework Process Context Layer (2026/04)
results.justification: Explicit Process Context Layer + Conflict Detection Engine — the candidate's own novelty claim concedes this is adjacent; the continuous-vs-snapshot distinction is implementation-level, same artefact.

results.title: AgentsNet 5 Distributed-Computing Primitives Consensus (2025/07)
results.justification: Distributed-computing primitives including consensus on shared state; functionally overlapping.

results.title: Voting or Consensus Multi-Agent (ACL 2025)
results.justification: Surveys 7 decision protocols including consensus; covers the consensus-mechanism space the candidate targets.

results.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration Mechanisms Survey (2025)
results.justification: Comprehensive survey of multi-agent collaboration mechanisms necessarily covers blackboard/shared-state variants.

results.title: Blackboard LLM Multi-Agent System bMAS
results.justification: DIRECT TWIN — explicitly describes 'every agent reads entirety of blackboard before acting; posts outputs as entries' which is the literal BAHAL-PROCESS-LAYER design.

verdict_sentence: FAIL — 8/8 results hit via both semantic (>=0.7) and functional (>=0.7) channels, with two direct twins (arxiv 2510.01285 and bMAS) implementing the exact append-only shared blackboard / read-before-act design that the BAHAL-PROCESS-LAYER proposal claims as novel.
